data.text: a machine that resembles a living creature in being capable of moving independently (as by walking or rolling on wheels) and performing complex actions (such as grasping and moving objects); often: such a machine built to resemble a human being or animal in appearance and behavior —often used before another noun
examples: Today, robots are operating on or around the Moon and Mars.

While science fiction robots have been capable of independent thought, emotions, even a little cooking and sewing, scientists are finding that endowing a mechanical being with even the most basic human functions is a monumental challenge.
As a sign of the times, paleontologists themselves have taken to … designing dinosaur robots and displays …
the use of robots in car manufacturing
… factories run by robots producing many replicas of one product.
a robot airplane
… seems to believe that professors are robots, students are superficial, grade-hungry receptacles …

data.text: a device that automatically performs complicated, often repetitive tasks (as in an industrial assembly line) —often used before another noun
examples: Today, robots are operating on or around the Moon and Mars.

enrichment.etymology: Czech, from robota compulsory labor; akin to Old High German arabeit trouble, Latin orbus orphaned
